picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The Cleveland Cavaliers are in a crucial Game 3 at home, trailing the series 0-2, creating a strong 'desperation angle' for the team. Cleveland boasts an impressive 6-1 home record this postseason, demonstrating a significant home-court advantage where their offensive rating elevates to 117.6 points per 100 possessions. The spread has moved from an opening of -1.5 to the current -2.5, indicating sharp money is favoring the Cavaliers to cover. For this pick to hit, the Cavaliers need to win by 3 points or more, which is a strong possibility given their motivation and historical home performance.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: Both the New York Knicks and Cleveland Cavaliers play at a notably slow pace, with the Knicks being the slowest team over the last 25 games and the Cavaliers the second slowest over the last 5 games. The Knicks possess an elite defense, ranking 5th in the NBA in opponent points per game (110.1) and 7th in defensive rating (113.3). In Game 2, the Cavaliers struggled significantly on offense, shooting just 39% from the field and a dismal 26% from three-point range, resulting in a low-scoring affair. Given the defensive intensity typical of a pivotal playoff game, combined with both teams' deliberate pace and Cleveland's recent offensive struggles, the Under is a high-confidence play.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks enter Game 3 with a commanding 2-0 series lead and are on an impressive nine-game playoff winning streak, with eight of those victories coming by double digits. They have also demonstrated strong performance on the road in the playoffs, holding a 4-1 record. Historically, the Knicks have dominated the Cavaliers in playoff series, winning all four previous matchups and holding a significant 14-2 head-to-head playoff advantage. Despite playing in a hostile road environment, the Knicks' superior recent form, defensive prowess, and overall series control make them a valuable underdog at +120.

The Montreal Canadiens have demonstrated significant recent success against the Hurricanes, winning their last five head-to-head matchups, including a decisive 6-2 victory in Game 1 of this series. Montreal also holds an impressive 12-2 record as road underdogs in their last 14 games and has not suffered back-to-back losses this postseason. Carolina's starting goaltender, Frederik Andersen, struggled in Game 1, allowing five goals on just 21 shots, which could be a vulnerability for the Hurricanes. These factors combined present a strong case for the Canadiens to pull off another upset.

Game 1 of this series saw a total of 8 goals, easily surpassing the 6.5 line. Montreal's offense has been performing well, with their top line finding its stride, while Carolina is expected to come out with increased offensive aggression at home to rebound from their Game 1 loss. Historical trends also support a higher-scoring game, as the 5.5 line has been covered in 6 of Montreal's last 10 games and 12 of Carolina's last 20 home games. With both teams likely pushing the pace, the Over 6.5 at plus money offers strong value.

Despite losing Game 1, the Carolina Hurricanes were previously on a nine-game winning streak, including sweeping their first two playoff series. They are a formidable home team, boasting a 7-1 record in their last eight home games, and will be highly motivated to deliver a strong bounce-back performance to even the series. While Montreal has shown resilience, the Hurricanes' overall dominance prior to Game 1 and their home-ice advantage suggest they are capable of a convincing victory to cover the spread.

Jake Evans is an active player for the Montreal Canadiens and has demonstrated a strong trend in his assist production following a win. In his last four games after a victory, Evans has consistently recorded over 0.5 assists, averaging 1.25 helpers per contest. Given Montreal's offensive momentum from Game 1 and Evans' consistent playmaking in favorable situations, this prop offers excellent value at +260 odds.

Ivan Demidov is confirmed active for the Montreal Canadiens and is positioned in a key offensive role, skating on the second attack line alongside productive players like Jake Evans and Alex Newhook. Crucially, Demidov also features on Montreal's primary powerplay unit, which provides him with ample opportunities to generate points from high-danger scoring areas. With the Canadiens' offense clicking and Demidov's significant role in their attack, he is well-placed to record at least one point in Game 2 at favorable plus-money odds.

The St. Louis Cardinals are a strong moneyline pick against the Cincinnati Reds, primarily due to the Reds' starting pitcher, Chris Paddack, who holds a concerning 7.07 ERA this season. The Cardinals' offense features Jordan Walker, who is performing exceptionally well with a .295 batting average and 13 home runs, providing significant offensive firepower. While Cincinnati is on a two-game winning streak, Paddack's struggles at home against a capable Cardinals lineup make St. Louis a favorable value. The Cardinals also boast a solid 15-8 road record, demonstrating their ability to win away from home.

The Chicago Cubs are a high-confidence moneyline pick, showing significant sharp money movement in their favor from an opening line of -143 to the current -162. The Cubs possess a superior pitching staff with a 4.07 ERA, notably better than the Astros' 5.28 ERA, and starter Colin Rea has a strong 2.66 ERA at home. The Astros have a poor 9-17 road record, making it challenging for them to compete against a motivated Cubs team at Wrigley Field, where Chicago's offense averages 4.85 runs per game. This combination of sharp line movement, pitching advantage, and home-field offensive prowess points to a Cubs victory.

Yordan Alvarez is an excellent player prop bet, given his outstanding offensive production this season, including 15 home runs and a 1.017 OPS. He faces Cubs starter Colin Rea, who has a tendency to allow hits, surrendering 10.15 hits per nine innings. Alvarez's impressive .601 slugging percentage makes him a strong candidate to achieve at least two total bases, especially at favorable plus-money odds. The Astros' lineup, despite their overall road record, maintains a respectable .267 batting average away from home, indicating opportunities for Alvarez to get on base and contribute.

The Cleveland Guardians are a high-confidence moneyline pick, riding an impressive 9-1 streak over their last ten games and seven consecutive wins. Despite facing Phillies ace Zack Wheeler, sharp money is leaning towards Cleveland, indicating a market overreaction to Wheeler's presence. The Guardians' bullpen has been exceptionally dominant, allowing only two earned runs over its past 18 innings, providing a significant late-game advantage. Furthermore, the Phillies have a poor 7-21 home run line record and have struggled in day games, adding to the Guardians' overall appeal.

The Arizona Diamondbacks are a high-confidence pick to cover the -1.5 spread against the Colorado Rockies. Arizona boasts a strong 16-10 home record and has been in excellent recent form, winning four of their last five games. They face Rockies starter Michael Lorenzen, who carries a concerning 7.03 ERA, presenting a significant advantage for the Diamondbacks' offense. Furthermore, Arizona's pitching rotation has shown dramatic improvement since early May, posting a 2.48 ERA over their last 16 starts. With the Rockies struggling on the road (10-17) and the Diamondbacks leading the season series 3-2, Arizona is well-positioned for a multi-run victory.

Chicago Fire FC is in strong form, boasting a 7-2-4 record and coming off back-to-back MLS wins, including a 2-0 away victory against CF Montréal. Conversely, Toronto FC is struggling, having lost successive MLS games and failing to win any of their last five matches. Historically, Chicago has not lost to Toronto in their past seven meetings, with their last encounter ending in a 2-2 draw. Given Chicago's current momentum and their historical dominance over Toronto, they are well-positioned to secure a home win.

FC Dallas enters this match in superior form, having won their last two MLS road games, including a 3-2 victory over San Jose Earthquakes. In contrast, Colorado Rapids have lost their last two home appearances and hold a poor recent record of 3 wins, 6 losses, and 1 draw in their last 10 league games. Furthermore, Dallas has consistently covered the +0.25 line in 7 of their last 10 games and in 2 consecutive road games, indicating their strength as road underdogs. This current form and betting trend favor FC Dallas for an upset victory.

The head-to-head history between Vancouver Whitecaps and San Diego FC strongly suggests a high-scoring match, with all 3 previous matchups exceeding 1.5 goals and 2 out of 3 going over 3.5 goals. San Diego FC's most recent home game was a 3-3 draw, demonstrating their propensity for open, high-scoring contests. Vancouver Whitecaps also possess a potent offense, having scored 30 goals in 13 games this season. These factors, combined with the favorable plus odds, make the Over 3.5 a confident pick.

The head-to-head record between LA Galaxy and Houston Dynamo FC frequently features goals, with 67% of their previous 33 meetings going over 2.5 goals and averaging 3.5 goals per match. The LA Galaxy is currently on an MLS-best scoring streak of 23 consecutive matches, and Gabriel Pec is in excellent form, contributing to five goals in their last three road games. Although the Galaxy's top scorer, João Klauss, is injured, Pec's current form and the historical high-scoring trend between these teams strongly indicate this match will exceed 2.5 goals.

New England Revolution, despite an average away record of 1-1-3, holds a significant historical advantage over Charlotte FC, having won 6 of their 9 previous encounters. This includes a recent 1-0 victory on May 2, 2026. The Revolution's overall form this season is 'Very Good' with an 8-1-4 record, placing them 6th in the MLS standings. Given their strong head-to-head record and solid overall performance, New England presents excellent value as an underdog at +290.
